introduction: Truco is a Brazilian card game for four players in two teams. Fast, loud, and built on bluffing. This site covers Paulista (São Paulo) and Mineiro (Minas Gerais) styles.
deck: 40 cards — A, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, 7, J, Q, K in each suit (8s, 9s, 10s removed). Ranking from lowest to highest: 4, 5, 6, 7, Q, J, K, A, 2, 3. Note: Queens rank below Jacks.
objective: Win a hand by taking 2 of 3 rounds.
Hands are normally worth 1 point — raise the stakes by calling Truco.
gameplay: Each player receives 3 cards per hand.
Each round, every player plays one card — highest card wins the round for their team.
Win 2 rounds to win the hand.
In rounds 2 and 3, you may play a card face-down (fechada). It can never win, but hides your hand.
tiedRounds: Equal-rank cards from opposing teams — the round is tied, neither team scores it.
After a tie, the next team to win any round wins the entire hand.
manilhasIntro: Manilhas are trump cards that beat all normal cards. Both versions share the same suit rank: Clubs > Hearts > Spades > Diamonds.

paulistaManilhas.title: Paulista — Vira
paulistaManilhas.description: After dealing, the dealer flips the top remaining card (the vira). Manilhas are the next rank up from the vira, in all four suits.
paulistaManilhas.example: Vira is 7 → all Queens become manilhas.

trucoIntro: Any player can call Truco on their turn to raise the hand's value. The opposing team must respond.
trucoResponses: Accept — play continues for the raised amount.
Raise — counter-raise to a higher value.
Forfeit — give up the current points.
trucoWarning: You cannot raise if accepting would bring the opponent to 12.

trucoVariations.mineiro: Mineiro: Truco applies to the current round only.
trucoVariations.paulista: Paulista: Truco applies to the entire hand. Partners may look at each other's cards before responding.

atEleven.title: At 11 — Mao de Onze
rules: The team at 11 sees their partner's cards before deciding.
Choose to play (hand worth 3 pts, no Truco) or forfeit 1 point and skip the hand.

ironHand.title: Iron Hand — Mao de Ferro
ironHand.description: When both teams reach 11, all cards are played face-down for all 3 rounds. No Truco. Winner takes the game.

tips: Win rounds 1 and 2 early — don't waste strong cards on a round 3 you don't need.
Bluff with Truco. A confident call on a weak hand can win points without ever playing.
Save manilhas for critical moments — don't burn them on rounds already won.
After a tied round, the very next win decides the hand.
At 11, look at your partner's cards carefully before deciding to play.
